title: eleocharis interstincta
intro: Eleocharis interstincta, also known as the lesser spike-rush, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family. Traditionally, the roots and stems of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eleocharis interstincta include its ability to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic to help alleviate water retention. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
